title: Top-N per partition via correlated subquery with window function
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.TopCommenters.top_n_per_category/1` that returns the top N commenters per category. The inner subquery joins comments with posts, groups by `{user_id, category_id}`, computes `total: count(c.id)` AND `rank: row_number() |> over(partition_by: p.category_id, order_by: [desc: count(c.id)])`. The outer query joins the subquery with User, filters `rank <= ^n`, selects `%{category_id, user_id, name, total_comments, rank}`, and orders `[asc: category_id, asc: rank]`. Uses named binding `as: :rank_row` for the subquery, `as: :commenter` for the User join. Write to `lib/my_app/blog/top_commenters.ex`.
tier_rationale: Integrates window functions, subquery-as-join-source, named bindings, pinned limit, and correct aggregate ordering. All pieces must work together — one of the most demanding query patterns.
